issues.description: challenge.json commonMistakes are generic boilerplate (colon after if/for/while, = vs ==, indentation) not specific to list basics. These are M01-level mistakes.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace with list-specific mistakes: e.g., off-by-one indexing, using parentheses instead of brackets, forgetting that indexes start at 0.

issues.description: challenge.json has only 1 test case with empty expectedOutput ('Code runs without errors'), making automated validation impossible.
issues.suggestedFix: Add 3-5 test cases verifying specific outputs like playlist count, first/last song, and average title length.

issues.description: challenge.json commonMistakes are generic boilerplate not specific to list methods.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace with list-method-specific mistakes: e.g., confusing append() with extend(), forgetting that sort() modifies in-place and returns None, using + instead of append() for single items.

issues.description: challenge.json has only 1 test case with empty expectedOutput.
issues.suggestedFix: Add test cases verifying specific list operations and their outputs.

findings.lessonTitle: List Slicing: Extract Portions of Lists

issues.description: challenge.json commonMistakes are generic boilerplate not specific to list slicing.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace with slicing-specific mistakes: e.g., confusing inclusive/exclusive end index, negative step direction, off-by-one in slice ranges.

issues.description: challenge.json has only 1 test case with empty expectedOutput.
issues.suggestedFix: Add test cases verifying specific slice outputs.

issues.description: Expected output hardcodes sys.getsizeof() byte values (List: 104 bytes, Tuple: 80 bytes, difference: 24 bytes). These values are platform- and Python-version-dependent; they will differ across Python 3.10/3.11/3.12/3.14 and 32-bit vs 64-bit systems, producing unexpected mismatches for learners.
issues.suggestedFix: Remove exact byte values from expected output or add a note that sizes vary by platform. Better: show relative comparison without exact numbers.

issues.description: In the Database Records example, the loop variable 'id' shadows the Python built-in id() function: 'for id, name, major, gpa in students'.
issues.suggestedFix: Rename to 'student_id' or 'sid' to avoid shadowing the built-in.

issues.description: Uses hardcoded date tuple (2024, 1, 15) - the year 2024 is already past.
issues.suggestedFix: Use a future or generic date, or use dynamic datetime.
issues.webResearchNote: Current year is 2026. The 2024 date reference is outdated. While not functionally broken, it makes the content feel stale. Recommend using a year-agnostic or more current reference.

issues.description: challenge.json commonMistakes are generic boilerplate not specific to tuples.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace with tuple-specific mistakes: e.g., forgetting the comma for single-element tuples, trying to modify a tuple, confusing tuple() constructor with parentheses grouping.

issues.description: challenge.json has only 1 test case with empty expectedOutput.
issues.suggestedFix: Add test cases verifying tuple unpacking, immutability handling, and conversion operations.

issues.description: Lesson difficulty is 'beginner' but list comprehensions (including nested comprehensions, conditional filtering, and generator expressions) are an intermediate-level concept that builds on loops, lists, and functions.
challenge.json commonMistakes are generic boilerplate not specific to list comprehensions.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace with comprehension-specific mistakes: e.g., wrong order of for/if clauses, trying to use elif in comprehension, overly complex nested comprehensions.

issues.description: challenge.json has only 1 test case with empty expectedOutput.
issues.suggestedFix: Add test cases verifying specific comprehension outputs.

issues.description: starter.py has multiple syntax errors that prevent it from running: unclosed list comprehensions (lines with 'averages = [', 'highly_rated = [', 'recent = [', 'recommendations = [', 'high_rated = len([', 'med_rated = len([', 'low_rated = len([') and an incomplete assignment ('avg =   # Calculate average'). Students cannot even parse the starter code to understand the structure.
issues.suggestedFix: Complete all list comprehensions with placeholder values (e.g., 'averages = []  # YOUR CODE: list comprehension'), or use pass/None as placeholders instead of syntactically broken expressions.

issues.description: challenge.json commonMistakes are generic boilerplate not specific to the mini-project content.
issues.suggestedFix: Replace with project-specific mistakes: e.g., incorrect average calculation, forgetting to handle empty lists, wrong tuple unpacking.

issues.description: challenge.json has only 1 test case with empty expectedOutput.
issues.suggestedFix: Add test cases verifying movie count, average calculations, and top 3 output format.
